name: Swiss-Sauced Broccoli
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the broccoli and remove any outer leaves and tough stalks.
2. Cut the broccoli stalks lengthwise into uniform spears, following the branching lines.
3. In a 1-quart casserole, combine the broccoli, water, and salt. Cook on high until tender, about 5 to 6 minutes.
4. Let the broccoli stand while preparing the sauce.
5. For the sauce, melt butter or margarine in a 1-cup measure on high for 30 to 45 seconds.
6. Stir in flour, salt, and pepper. Add milk and cook until thickened and bubbly, stirring every 30 seconds.
7. Remove from heat and stir in shredded Swiss cheese until melted.
8. Drain the broccoli and serve the sauce over top.
